name: AI-adoption report
description: Pair flow-metrics JSON outputs, subtract, and render a Markdown comparison report in one of three modes: baseline (one scope, two windows — pre-AI vs current), cohort (within-window AI-cohort vs control split that flow-metrics already computed via --cohort-jql), and program (roll up many scopes for a single window). Read-only: consumes flow-metrics JSON files, never invokes flow-metrics / jira / jira-align, and writes only the Markdown report and an optional JSON sidecar. Stdlib only.

deps._runtime_contract: This skill consumes flow-metrics JSON FILES, not the flow-metrics skill at runtime. It makes no upstream skill invocations — the read-only contract tests in tests/test_t9_packaging.py enforce zero subprocess / Popen / os.spawn / os.system calls during a report run. deps.skills is [] for that reason. Generating fresh input JSONs is a separate step the user runs against the flow-metrics skill first.
deps._pip_rationale: No deps.pip entry: v1 is stdlib only. Same posture as flow-metrics — argparse, json, statistics, pathlib, tempfile, datetime are sufficient. The output schema is enforced by hand-written serialisation rules (see scripts/ai_adoption_report/render.py + scripts/ai_adoption_report/write.py), not a jsonschema validator.

input.arguments: Required: subcommand MODE (one of baseline / cohort / program) plus --output FILE. baseline mode also requires --baseline PATH --current PATH; cohort mode requires --input PATH; program mode requires --inputs DIR --window FROM..TO. Common optional flags: --format {markdown,json,both} (default both), --overwrite (replace existing output without prompting), --title TITLE, --verbose. Mode-specific optional flags: --include-cohort-breakdown (baseline + program).

env.AI_ADOPTION_REPORT_GENERATED_AT: Optional. When set, the skill uses this ISO-8601 string verbatim for the report's meta.generated_at instead of reading the runtime clock. Intended for deterministic-build tests and golden-file diffs (T9 golden tests rely on this). Unset in normal operation.

input._invocation_note: CLI is invoked as `python -m ai_adoption_report MODE ...` (the package's __main__.py shims to ai_adoption_report.main). Resolving the package as importable Python is the user's responsibility (typically PYTHONPATH pointed at scripts/).

artifacts: <--output> (default extension .md): canonical Markdown report with fixed section order — title, mode-specific header line, ## Summary, ## Metric deltas, ## Per-scope rows (program mode only), ## Cohort breakdown (when --include-cohort-breakdown), ## Notes, ## Provenance.

<--output with .md replaced by .json> (or <--output>.json if no extension): compact JSON sidecar — meta.skill_version, per-input provenance, the full deltas block in canonical metric order, optional per_scope (program mode), optional cohort_breakdown, sorted notes array. Object keys are codepoint-sorted at every level except deltas; floats are rounded to 4 decimal places.

Both files written atomically via tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile + os.replace within --output's parent directory. Pre-flight collision check fails the run before any write when either target exists and --overwrite was not supplied.
